stream_samples.text: Smart Sync just re-downloaded 28GB of online-only files overnight. macbook ran out of disk before standup. third time this month.
Camera upload created 4,200 duplicate photos on my Pixel after the 380.x mobile update. dedupe job ran for 6 hours and missed half.
[#SYNC-6611 P1] enterprise customer: Smart Sync hydrated 1.2TB across 340 seats unprompted, blew their bandwidth budget for the month
Smart Sync hydration regression confirmed in 200.4.x desktop client — file_state cache invalidates on focus event, triggers full re-pull. fix in 200.4.7 nightly.
share link with 'editor' permission keeps reverting to 'commenter' for external collaborators after 24h. nobody changed it.
Paper migration deadline is in 6 weeks and the export keeps dropping table formatting and inline media. raised 2 tickets, no fix ETA.
[#FILE-4882 P2] 11GB video upload stalls at 5.1GB on every retry. chunked uploader times out before resume token refreshes.
Dropbox Dash returns my own draft notes ahead of the canonical confluence page everyone references. ranking feels off.
iOS app keeps prompting 'free up space' even with 80GB available. then it deletes my offline-pinned folders silently.
support load: 41% of P1s this week are Smart Sync re-hydration. enterprise CSMs are escalating, two accounts paused renewal conversations.
Replay timestamp comments are landing 2-3 seconds off the actual frame after the new transcoder rolled out. video review is unusable for cuts.
Share link with password expires after 7 days even when 'never expire' is set. team is regenerating links daily for client portals.
Dropbox Dash actually pulled the right Notion doc on the first query today. when it works it feels magical.
is there a way to disable Smart Sync entirely on managed devices? our IT keeps getting bandwidth tickets and we just want plain sync
[smart-sync] file_state cache key collides on case-insensitive filesystems, causes spurious hydration on rename-to-different-case

clusters.title: Paper export drops formatting on migration
Replay timestamp comment drift after transcode

top_epic.title: Stop Smart Sync from re-hydrating online-only files
top_epic.rationale: 2,310 signals across Zendesk, X and Slack point to the same regression: Smart Sync silently re-downloads online-only files after the 200.4.x desktop client rollout. The file_state cache invalidates on focus events and on case-insensitive filesystem renames, triggering full re-pulls. One enterprise account hydrated 1.2TB across 340 seats unprompted; 41% of this week's P1 tickets are this exact failure, and two accounts have paused renewal conversations.
top_epic.verdict: Trust-critical — the core promise of Smart Sync is that online-only stays online-only.
top_epic.recommendation: Fix file_state cache invalidation, add a hard rate-limit on hydration per device per hour, and ship a CSM-facing dashboard so enterprise admins can see and cap re-hydration before bandwidth bills land.
acceptance_criteria: file_state cache survives focus events and case-rename on APFS/NTFS
Admin console shows per-device hydration in last 24h with cap controls
Telemetry event smartsync.hydrate.unexpected emitted with root cause
